description: Open-source Python library for composable document data extraction: load, preprocess, OCR, LLM parse, store with vector search. Published as `billfox` on PyPI with a `billfox` CLI.

referenceCodebase.instruction: You MUST reference the existing codebase at ../billfox-app/api/src for implementation inspiration. This codebase contains proven patterns and working code that should be ported/adapted into this new library. Always read and understand the relevant source files before implementing each user story. Key directories to reference: ai/ (AI agents, YOLO, embeddings), store/ (SQLite, search pipeline, vector types), utils/ (storage, helpers), pipelines/ (pipeline patterns), models/ (data models), schemas/ (Pydantic schemas).

userStories.title: Project scaffold and core types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with pyproject.toml, core types, and all protocol definitions so every subsequent module has a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: New repository initialised with src/billfox/ layout
pyproject.toml uses hatch build backend, package name 'billfox', requires-python >=3.11
Core dependency is only pydantic>=2.0
Optional extras defined: mistral, yolo, llm, openai, store, cli, all, dev
_version.py with version 0.1.0
py.typed PEP 561 marker file present
_types.py defines Document, Page, ExtractionResult, SearchResult as frozen dataclasses
source/_base.py defines DocumentSource protocol with async load(uri) -> Document
preprocess/_base.py defines Preprocessor protocol with async process(doc) -> Document
extract/_base.py defines Extractor protocol with async extract(doc) -> ExtractionResult
parse/_base.py defines Parser[T] protocol with async parse(markdown) -> T
embed/_base.py defines Embedder protocol with async embed(texts) -> list[list[float]] and dimensions property
store/_base.py defines DocumentStore[T] protocol with save/get/search/delete methods
All protocols are @runtime_checkable
Each subpackage has __init__.py re-exporting public names
billfox/__init__.py re-exports Pipeline, Document, ExtractionResult, SearchResult
Makefile with targets: test, lint, format, typecheck
ruff and mypy config in pyproject.toml
LICENSE (MIT), CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md present
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for all other stories. No external dependencies beyond pydantic.

userStories.title: Source module — local file loading
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to load documents from the local filesystem so I can process images and PDFs.
acceptanceCriteria: source/local.py implements LocalFileSource matching DocumentSource protocol
load(uri) reads file bytes and detects MIME type from file extension
Supported MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/tiff, image/heic, application/pdf
Returns Document(content=bytes, mime_type=str, source_uri=str, metadata={})
Raises FileNotFoundError for missing files
Raises ValueError for unsupported MIME types
Unit tests cover: valid image, valid PDF, missing file, unsupported extension
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple module. Reference Billfox pattern: api/src/utils/storage.py for file loading.

userStories.title: Preprocessing — YOLO document cropping
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to crop documents using YOLO object detection so OCR accuracy improves.
acceptanceCriteria: preprocess/yolo.py implements YOLOPreprocessor matching Preprocessor protocol
Constructor accepts model_path: str for user-provided ONNX model
Uses ONNX Runtime for inference (lazy-imported with clear error if missing)
Ported from api/src/ai/yolo/receipt_onnx.py: _preprocess, _postprocess, _nms, _compute_iou, _compute_containment, crop_detections_onnx
Renamed from receipt-specific to generic document naming
process(document) returns new Document with cropped image bytes
If no detections found, returns original document unchanged
Only processes image MIME types; passes PDFs through unchanged
Configurable confidence threshold (default 0.25) and image size (default 640)
Unit tests with mocked ONNX session
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from api/src/ai/yolo/receipt_onnx.py. Rename receipt→document. Accept user model path.

userStories.title: Preprocessing — resize and chain
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to resize images and chain multiple preprocessors for flexible preprocessing pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: preprocess/resize.py implements ResizePreprocessor matching Preprocessor protocol
Accepts max_side: int (default 1024), maintains aspect ratio
Ported from api/src/ai/yolo/image.py: resize_keep_aspect logic
Only processes image MIME types; passes PDFs through unchanged
preprocess/chain.py implements PreprocessorChain matching Preprocessor protocol
Accepts preprocessors: list[Preprocessor], applies them sequentially
Unit tests for resize (aspect ratio preserved, max dimension respected)
Unit tests for chain (order matters, empty chain returns original)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port resize from api/src/ai/yolo/image.py. Chain is new composition utility.

userStories.title: Extraction — Mistral OCR
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to convert images and PDFs to markdown using Mistral OCR API.
acceptanceCriteria: extract/mistral.py implements MistralExtractor matching Extractor protocol
Constructor accepts optional api_key and model (default mistral-ocr-latest)
Falls back to MISTRAL_API_KEY env var if no api_key provided
Raises clear RuntimeError if no API key available
Ported from api/src/ai/agents/expense/mistral.py: to_data_url, validate_mime, OCR call logic
Correctly branches image_url vs document_url based on MIME type
Returns ExtractionResult(markdown=str, pages=list[Page], metadata={})
mistralai is lazy-imported with clear install instructions on ImportError
Unit tests with mocked Mistral client (verify request format, response parsing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from api/src/ai/agents/expense/mistral.py. Key detail: image_url vs document_url branching.

userStories.title: Parsing — LLM structured extraction
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to parse markdown into any Pydantic model using an LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: parse/llm.py implements LLMParser[T] matching Parser[T] protocol
Constructor accepts: model (str like 'openai:gpt-4.1'), output_type (type[T] any Pydantic BaseModel), system_prompt (str), optional retries (default 1)
Uses pydantic-ai Agent under the hood with structured output
parse(markdown) returns an instance of T
pydantic-ai is lazy-imported with clear install instructions on ImportError
Works with any LLM provider supported by pydantic-ai
Unit tests with mocked pydantic-ai agent
Test with a sample Pydantic model (e.g. Invoice with vendor_name, total, date)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Generic wrapper around pydantic-ai. User provides output_type and system_prompt.

userStories.title: Pipeline compositor
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to wire all stages together in a single Pipeline for end-to-end document processing.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline.py implements Pipeline[T] as a generic dataclass
Constructor accepts: source, extractor, parser, optional preprocessors list, optional store
run(uri, document_id=None) executes: load -> preprocess -> extract -> parse -> store -> return T
extract_only(uri) executes: load -> preprocess -> extract -> return ExtractionResult
Pipeline works without preprocessors (empty list)
Pipeline works without store (store=None)
Store only called when both store and document_id are provided
Integration tests with all stages mocked, verifying call order
Test extract_only skips parse and store
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Composes all modules. Each stage is independently swappable.

userStories.title: Embedding module — OpenAI
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to generate text embeddings using OpenAI for semantic search.
acceptanceCriteria: embed/openai.py implements OpenAIEmbedder matching Embedder protocol
Constructor accepts optional api_key and model (default text-embedding-3-small)
Falls back to OPENAI_API_KEY env var
embed(texts) returns list[list[float]] — one embedding per input text
dimensions property returns 1536 for text-embedding-3-small
Parallel embedding generation via asyncio.gather
Includes encode_vector and decode_vector utility functions (base64 float packing)
openai is lazy-imported with clear install instructions on ImportError
Unit tests with mocked OpenAI client
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from api/src/ai/embeddings.py. Wrap in Embedder protocol.

userStories.title: Storage — SQLite schema and CRUD
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to store extracted data in SQLite so I can persist and retrieve documents.
acceptanceCriteria: store/_schema.py defines SQLAlchemy models: documents table (id, schema_name, data_json, source_uri, raw_markdown, created_at, updated_at)
store/_schema.py defines document_embeddings table (id, document_id FK, field_name, text_content, UNIQUE on document_id+field_name)
store/_vector.py implements VectorType (SQLite-only: LargeBinary with float32 struct packing)
Ported from api/src/store/column_types.py — SQLite branches only, drop PostgreSQL
store/sqlite.py implements SQLiteDocumentStore[T] matching DocumentStore[T] protocol
Constructor accepts: db_path, schema (type[T]), optional embedder, optional embed_fields
Auto-creates tables on first use (including sqlite-vec virtual table and FTS5 table)
SQLite WAL mode, foreign keys ON, busy_timeout=5000 configured
sqlite-vec loaded via sqlite_vec.load() with graceful fallback if unavailable
save(document_id, data) stores Pydantic model as JSON, syncs embeddings to virtual tables
get(document_id) deserialises JSON back to Pydantic model, returns None if not found
delete(document_id) cascades to embeddings
sqlalchemy, aiosqlite, sqlite-vec lazy-imported with clear error messages
Integration tests with in-memory SQLite: save, get, delete round-trip
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port VectorType from api/src/store/column_types.py (SQLite-only). Port sqlite-vec setup from api/src/store/db.py.

userStories.title: Storage — hybrid search
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to search stored documents using hybrid BM25 + vector search with RRF fusion.
acceptanceCriteria: store/_search.py implements hybrid search pipeline
BM25 search via FTS5 virtual table returning (document_id, bm25_score) tuples
Vector KNN search via sqlite-vec returning (document_id, cosine_similarity) tuples
normalize_scores() function ported directly from api/src/store/search_pipeline.py
apply_hybrid_fusion() function ported directly — 60% normalised + 40% RRF (k=60)
Tie-breaking by created_at recency
SearchResult includes signals dict for explainability (bm25, vector, rrf, final scores)
search(query, limit=20) on SQLiteDocumentStore performs hybrid search
If embedder available: runs both BM25 + vector signals
If no embedder: falls back to BM25-only search
sqlite_vec_knn_search helper ported from api/src/store/search.py
sync_vector_to_virtual_table helper ported from api/src/store/search.py
Integration tests with in-memory SQLite: store documents, search, verify ranking
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port from api/src/store/search_pipeline.py (normalize_scores, apply_hybrid_fusion) and api/src/store/search.py (sqlite_vec_knn_search). Generalise from expense-specific to generic.

userStories.title: CLI — extract and parse commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want billfox extract and billfox parse commands to process documents from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/app.py implements Typer application
cli/__main__.py enables python -m billfox
pyproject.toml registers billfox script entry point: billfox = billfox.cli:app
billfox extract <file> runs OCR and outputs markdown to stdout or --output file
extract flags: --extractor (default mistral), --preprocess (comma-separated), --api-key
billfox parse <file> runs full pipeline and outputs JSON to stdout or --output file
parse flags: --schema (path:ClassName), --model (default openai:gpt-4.1), --prompt, --store (db path), --preprocess
--schema dynamically loads Pydantic model from Python file using importlib
--json flag outputs machine-readable JSON
--verbose flag enables debug output
billfox --help and billfox extract --help work correctly
typer and rich lazy-imported with clear error messages
Unit tests using Typer test runner for extract and parse commands
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLI entry point is 'billfox'. Dynamic schema loading via importlib.

userStories.title: CLI — search and config commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want billfox search and billfox config commands to query stored documents and manage settings.
acceptanceCriteria: billfox search <query> searches stored documents
search flags: --db (required), --limit (default 20), --mode (hybrid/vector/bm25), --json
Search results displayed as formatted table (rich) or JSON with --json flag
billfox config set <key> <value> writes to ~/.billfox/config.toml
billfox config get <key> reads from config file
billfox config list shows all config values
Config supports api_keys.mistral, api_keys.openai, default extractor, default model
Config directory auto-created if missing
billfox search --help and billfox config --help work
Unit tests for search and config commands
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Config stored at ~/.billfox/config.toml. Uses tomllib (stdlib) for reading, tomli-w for writing.

userStories.title: Documentation and README
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating the library, I want clear documentation so I can understand and adopt billfox quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md with: badges, one-line description, install instructions, 3 quick-start examples (OCR-only, full pipeline, CLI), architecture diagram (ASCII), link to docs
docs/ directory with mkdocs-material config (mkdocs.yml)
Docs pages: getting-started.md, custom-extractor.md, custom-preprocessor.md, storage-and-search.md
CONTRIBUTING.md with dev setup, running tests, code style, PR process
CHANGELOG.md with v0.1.0 entry listing all features
All public APIs have docstrings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Focus on practical examples. Architecture diagram should show the pipeline stages.

userStories.title: CI and integration verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI and integration tests to verify the full pipeline works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml for Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13 test matrix
CI runs: pytest, mypy --strict, ruff check
Integration test: full pipeline with mocked LLM + mocked OCR + real in-memory SQLite
Integration test: extract_only flow (source -> preprocess -> extract)
Integration test: store -> search round-trip with synthetic embeddings
Integration test: hybrid search returns results ranked by RRF fusion
Import isolation test: import billfox works with only pydantic installed
Extras isolation test: importing billfox.extract.mistral without mistralai gives clear ImportError
CLI smoke test: billfox --help exits 0
All tests pass across Python 3.11/3.12/3.13
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final verification story. All previous stories must pass before this one.
